commit.message:
fix: use spawn.pickSpawnPoint for correct map default spawn position

Previous implementation used findClassObjects('SpawnSphere') which
never found anything because BeamNG spawn points are named scene
objects, not SpawnSphere class instances.

Now mirrors BeamNG's own spawn.pickSpawnPoint('player') fallback chain
(same order as levels.lua and spawn.lua):
  1. spawn.pickSpawnPoint('player')  — BeamNG's own function, preferred
  2. setSpawnpoint.loadDefaultSpawnpoint() → scenetree.findObject(name)
  3. PlayerSpawnPoints / PlayerDropPoints group → random member
  4. DefaultPlayerSpawnSphere (legacy compatibility)
  5. Current player vehicle position
  6. Hard-coded (0, 0, 130) last resort — should never be reached

Also: spawnCurrentVehicleType calls getDefaultSpawnPos internally
when no pos is supplied, so no hard-coded coordinates are needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: prefer current vehicle, use map default spawn on respawn

- New Lua command getDefaultSpawnPos: finds first SpawnSphere in the
  scene and returns its position + configurable Z offset (default +0.5).
  Falls back to current player vehicle pos, then hard-coded origin.

- spawnCurrentVehicleType now calls getDefaultSpawnPos internally when
  no explicit pos is provided -- no more hard-coded coordinates needed.

- drive_forward.py Step 4 reworked:
  1. Try usePlayerVehicle first -- if a vehicle is already in the scene,
     use it directly (no spawn, no interruption for the user).
  2. Only if that fails: query getDefaultSpawnPos from Lua, then call
     spawnCurrentVehicleType with that position.
  3. SPAWN_POSITIONS dict kept as last-resort static fallback only.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add AeroSpace tiling WM and SketchyBar status bar with machine-gated workspace layouts (#38)

## Summary

A ground-up macOS tiling-window + status bar setup built on AeroSpace,
SketchyBar, and JankyBorders, plus the surrounding cleanup it forced on
the rest of the repo. Started as "install AeroSpace and maybe theme it a
bit" and grew into a full menu-bar replacement, a rethink of how GitHub
MCP auth flows, machine-wide `defaults` captured into chezmoi, and a
pile of review cycles that shook out real bugs.

This branch has 23 commits across ~2,700 net insertions. The PR body
below is ordered by theme, not chronologically — the commit log tells
the churn story faithfully.

---

## AeroSpace — tiling on SIP-on macOS

**Why AeroSpace over yabai:** AeroSpace specifically doesn't require
`csrutil disable`. Viable for a SOC-locked work machine, where anything
touching SIP is a nonstarter.

**Keybinding layout.** Converged on `alt` + hjkl for focus, `alt-shift`
+ hjkl for window movement, `alt-1..9` for workspaces, `alt-shift-1..9`
for "shove this window to workspace N + follow it". Three modes (`main`
/ `resize` / `service`), following vim conventions. Zero overlap with:
- tmux (`ctrl-space` prefix)
- Neovim / IdeaVim (`space` leader)
- zsh (`alt-arrows` for z4h directory nav)
- IntelliJ (after we deleted the `alt-j`/`alt-k` multi-cursor bindings
from `dot_ideavimrc` — the native `ctrl-g` / `ctrl-shift-g` /
`ctrl-cmd-g` shortcuts do the same thing)

**Machine-type gating.** Early drafts kept AeroSpace personal-only. That
got walked back partway through — both machines now get the full setup
(same keybindings, same bar, same modes), and only the
`on-window-detected` workspace-assignment rules differ per machine. The
work-machine rule block is currently empty, ready to populate when I
spend a day on it there.

**Workspace 5 "comms" layout.**
`~/.config/aerospace/layouts/workspace-5-comms.sh` does the
Mail-over-Calendar stack on the left with Messages full-height on the
right via `flatten-workspace-tree` + `join-with up` + `balance-sizes`.
Took two revisions: the first crashed under `set -euo pipefail` when
Calendar wasn't running (grep returns 1 on no match), and the second
used `grep -i` which would match "Notion Calendar" too. Current form
uses awk exact-match on the app-name field.

**`on-focus-changed` bordersrc dance.** Hides the JankyBorders frame
(width=0) when a workspace has only one window — visually the window is
already "the focused thing," no need for a box around it. Restores to
3px when multiple windows are tiled. The conditional has to run through
an explicit `/bin/bash -c` wrapper because `exec-and-forget` execs its
argument directly without a shell, so `[ ] && ... || ...` and `$(...)`
don't expand otherwise.

---

## SketchyBar — built from scratch, not themed

There was no prior SketchyBar config to build on — this whole tree is
new.

**Theme.** Everforest Dark Hard, matching the tmux statusline and
Ghostty background already in the repo. Colors and fonts exported from
`sketchybarrc` so item scripts inherit them.

**Left group — workspace indicators.** Nine brackets, one per workspace,
each containing the number + up to 5 per-app colored icons via
[sketchybar-app-font](https://github.com/kvndrsslr/sketchybar-app-font)
(700+ glyphs). A manual brand-color table in `plugins/aerospace.sh` maps
common apps to their house colors (Ghostty teal, Firefox orange,
IntelliJ orange, Slack aubergine, Obsidian purple, etc.); everything
else falls through to a neutral gray. Unfocused workspaces dim all their
icons to gray — color is a focus signal.

**The 9× fanout story.** First attempt gave each of the 9 workspace
items its own `script=` and its own `--subscribe` to
`aerospace_workspace_change front_app_switched`. Every focus change
spawned 9 parallel subprocesses, each sourcing the 1,400-line
`icon_map.sh`. Reviewer caught it; first mitigation added an early-exit
in the plugin for unfocused workspaces (reduced *work* but still 9
forks). Second pass rewrote it as a **controller pattern**: a single
hidden `workspaces.controller` item subscribes to both events,
`plugins/aerospace.sh` fires once per event, runs one `aerospace
list-windows --all` query, and emits one batched `sketchybar --set ...`
invocation that updates every workspace. Verified via a probe log: 1
invocation per event, down from 9.

**Right group — status cluster.** Clock with seconds + tz, calendar with
weekday, battery with Material Design icons + time-to-full/empty, Wi-Fi
connectivity, volume mute indicator. All inside a single bracket so they
look like one chip. Hovering the clock/calendar pops a UTC time+date
dropdown; UTC labels are computed lazily on `mouse.entered` instead of
on every 1s tick.

**Bracket padding symmetry.** Workspaces start with 6px on the left
(`icon.padding_left` on the number) but what's on the right depends on
how many apps are tiled. Fixed with an invisible "end cap" item always
included in each bracket — `padding_left=5` on the cap +
`icon.padding_right=2` on the preceding element gives a constant 7px
right edge that matches the 6px left (+1 for the glyph's visual
center-of-mass).

---

## JankyBorders — focused-window frames

Landed with PR #39 earlier in this branch. Everforest green for focused
/ gray for unfocused. `hidpi=on` for crisp 1-physical-pixel edges on
Retina.

**Width iteration.** Went 5px → 6px → 3px → 2px → 3px based on
side-by-side feedback. The aerospace `gaps.inner` and `gaps.outer` have
to match the border width exactly or adjacent windows' borders draw into
the same pixel strip (overlapping colors) and edge borders get clipped
by the screen. Both values are 3px now, invariant documented in both
files.

**Corner-radius mismatch.** macOS 26 changed window chrome geometry and
JankyBorders' `style=round` auto-detection hasn't caught up. Not fixable
locally — no manual radius knob in the JB CLI. Live with it or switch to
`style=square`.

---

## macOS defaults — from live machine state to chezmoi

`.chezmoiscripts/darwin/run_onchange_configure-macos-defaults.sh`
captures the dozen-or-so tweaks I actually use: Dark mode, fast key
repeat (`InitialKeyRepeat=15`, `KeyRepeat=2`),
`ApplePressAndHoldEnabled=false` so vim-style key repeat works in every
text field, Dock on the left at `tilesize=40` with `autohide-delay=1.0`
+ slower animation (`autohide-time-modifier=0.6`) to stop edge
misclicks, Finder list view default, `~/Desktop/screenshots` for screen
captures, Activity Monitor "All Processes" default, trackpad tap-click
off, 4-finger swipes for Mission Control. Every key is a stock Apple
`defaults` key — same namespace System Settings writes — which keeps it
safe on SOC-locked machines.

**Explicitly not managed:** anything TCC-protected
(`com.apple.universalaccess` can't be written without Full Disk Access
even via `sudo`) — documented inline as one-time manual setup.

**Dock `persistent-apps` / `persistent-others` deliberately skipped:**
those contain per-machine binary `CFURLBookmark` blobs and shouldn't
ride in a portable dotfile.

---

## GitHub MCP — migration off the archived package

The config started as `@modelcontextprotocol/server-github` with
`"GITHUB_TOKEN": "${GITHUB_TOKEN}"`. Three things were wrong:

1. That package reads `GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN`, **not**
`GITHUB_TOKEN` — so auth was silently falling through to unauthenticated
rate limits.
2. `${GITHUB_TOKEN}` is expanded by the MCP client at spawn time, not by
`mcp_sync` at apply time. Once the shell profile dropped the `export
GITHUB_TOKEN=...` line (it was 80% of shell startup), it expanded to
`""`.
3. The package is archived — development moved to
[github/github-mcp-server](https://github.com/github/github-mcp-server).

Swapped to the official Homebrew `github-mcp-server` binary + a `sh -c`
wrapper that sources the PAT from `gh auth token` at spawn time:

```json
"args": [
  "-c",
  "TOKEN=\"$(gh auth token 2>/dev/null)\"; if [ -z \"$TOKEN\" ]; then echo 'github-mcp-server: gh auth token returned empty; run `gh auth login` to authenticate.' >&2; exit 1; fi; exec env GITHUB_PERSONAL_ACCESS_TOKEN=\"$TOKEN\" github-mcp-server stdio"
]
```

- No long-lived env var. `gh` owns the token lifecycle (macOS keychain
via `gh auth login`).
- Fails fast with a useful message if `gh` isn't authed, instead of
silently serving unauthenticated.
- Verified end-to-end: initialize handshake + `tools/call(get_me)`
returned live user identity from the GitHub API.

All 11 downstream tool configs regenerated via `mcp_sync`; zero stale
`GITHUB_TOKEN` references in the deployed tree.

---

## Bash 5 upgrade on the controller

Verified by probe script that sketchybar's spawned children inherit
`/opt/homebrew/bin` on `PATH`, so `#!/usr/bin/env bash` resolves to brew
bash 5.3.9 (not the `/bin/bash` 3.2 that Apple still ships on macOS 26).
Used it to clean up the controller:

- `declare -A APPS_BY_WS` — hash-based dedupe of apps per workspace,
O(n), no per-workspace `sort -u` subshell
- `${1,,}` native lowercase — drops the `echo "$1" | tr '[:upper:]'
'[:lower:]'` subshell from `color_for_app`, which fires on every
app-icon slot update
- `mapfile -t`, `[[ ]]`, `(( ))` throughout
- Hard `BASH_VERSINFO[0] < 4` check at the top so a broken PATH fails
loud instead of silently dropping updates

**Caught a real regression in the process.** `((i++))` + `set -e` +
`i=0` → post-increment returns the old value → arithmetic context reads
0 as false → script exits with status 1. The controller was silently
dying on the first workspace it processed, leaving the bar in whatever
state the previous version left behind. Fixed by switching to
`i=$((i+1))` (arithmetic assignment is always status 0). Ironic because
the bug was caught *because* `set -euo pipefail` was in place — case
study for the CLAUDE.md rule.

---

## Strict mode across the tree

Self-review + code-review surfaced that 13 new sketchybar/borders
scripts were missing `set -euo pipefail`, violating the explicit
CLAUDE.md style rule. Added it to all of them, with small defensive
tweaks where needed:

- `plugins/battery.sh` — `|| true` on `grep 'AC Power'` and `grep -Eo
'\d+:\d+'` pipelines that legitimately miss when not charging or no time
estimate is available
- `plugins/clock.sh` — `case "${SENDER:-}"` because `update_freq=1`
ticks set `SENDER=routine` but a forced fire can leave it unset
- `plugins/front_app.sh` — `[ -n "${INFO:-}" ]` for the same reason on
`INFO`
- `plugins/wifi.sh` — `|| true` on the networksetup | awk pipeline
because networksetup can exit non-zero on wake or when the Wi-Fi service
isn't listed

Tested by:
- Direct invocation of each plugin with its expected env (NAME / SENDER
/ INFO)
- Firing real sketchybar events: `power_source_change`, `wifi_change`,
`volume_change`, `front_app_switched`, `aerospace_workspace_change`,
plus `routine` update_freq ticks
- 9 consecutive `aerospace workspace N` transitions
- Cold sketchybar restart (pkill + relaunch) to confirm `sketchybarrc`
itself survives strict mode

17 probed invocations across 5 plugins; zero script deaths, all exit 0,
bar tracks state correctly.

---

## Wi-Fi detection iteration

Three revs:
1. `ipconfig getifaddr en0` — broke the reviewer's test on desktops
where en0 is Ethernet
2. `networksetup -listallhardwareports | awk '/Hardware Port: Wi-Fi/
...'` + `${WIFI_IF:-en0}` fallback — robust but two subprocesses and an
"AirPort" legacy fallback from OS X 10.6
3. `networksetup -getinfo Wi-Fi | awk '/^IP address:/ ...'` — one
subprocess, asks macOS directly for the Wi-Fi service state, handles
connected / on-but-unconnected / off uniformly via the empty-IP check

Settled on #3 once the instruction was "optimize for macOS 26, don't
preserve legacy."

---

## aws-sso-cli cleanup

`work-shell-functions.zsh` carried a complete `aws-sso-cli` integration
block — login functions, profile completion, precmd hook to defer
completion registration until `compinit` is ready. That tool is being
retired in this repo in favor of the in-progress `aws_config_gen` Python
tool, so the whole block got stripped (137 lines). Kept the generic
`get_assumed_role_credentials` STS helper with its docstring updated.

Two aws-sso references remain and are **intentionally out of scope** of
this PR (see stevencarpenter/dotfiles#40): the Powerlevel10k
AWS-indicator trigger in `dot_p10k.zsh` and the `${AWS_SSO_PROFILE}` env
expansion in `dot_config/mcp/machine/work.json`. Both belong with the
`aws_config_gen` cutover when that lands.

---

## Docs

- `docs/aerospace-cheatsheet.md` — keybinding reference, workspace map,
common workflows. Formatted for Zed's markdown preview (which is where I
read it from workspace 9).
- `docs/ai-tools/mcp-setup.md` — updated the GitHub MCP section to
reflect the `gh auth token` wrapper instead of the old env-var flow, and
added a callout on where auth actually lives now (macOS keychain).
- `README.md` — dropped `GITHUB_TOKEN` from the required-env-var list
with a note explaining why.

---

## Review history (for future me)

Seven rounds of review fed into this branch:
- Two [Copilot
reviewer](https://github.com/apps/copilot-pull-request-reviewer) passes
(initial + after changes)
- One [ChatGPT Codex](https://github.com/apps/chatgpt-codex-connector)
review
- One internal `superpowers:code-reviewer` subagent pass
- One formal `/code-review:code-review` pass that dispatched 5 parallel
Sonnet agents (CLAUDE.md compliance, shallow bug scan, git history,
prior PR carryover, in-code comment invariants) and scored each finding
before posting
- Two self-reviews that caught things the bots missed (the 9× fanout
optimization direction; the aws-sso cleanup)

Every finding that met the confidence threshold was addressed in-branch.
The commits titled "Address ... review ..." are the fixup landing
points.

---

## Test plan

- [x] `chezmoi diff` shows expected changes for the current machine type
- [x] `chezmoi apply -v` deploys everything, including the new
`run_onchange_configure-macos-defaults.sh`
- [x] AeroSpace launches and tiles (grant Accessibility on first launch)
- [x] `alt-h/j/k/l` navigates windows; `alt-1..9` switches workspaces;
`alt-shift-N` moves current window to workspace N
- [x] SketchyBar replaces the native menu bar; all 9 workspace
indicators + right-side status cluster render
- [x] Workspace assignments auto-route apps on open (`com.apple.mail` →
workspace 5, `md.obsidian` → workspace 7, etc.)
- [x] `alt-shift-;` then `5` arranges the Mail/Calendar/Messages comms
layout
- [x] Hovering clock/calendar shows the UTC popup
- [x] JankyBorders shows green border on focused window, gray on others,
hidden when a workspace is solo
- [x] No keybinding conflicts with tmux / Neovim / IdeaVim / zsh
- [x] GitHub MCP works from Claude Code: a `get_me` call returns the
live identity
- [x] Work machine gets the shared AeroSpace + SketchyBar + JankyBorders
setup with no personal workspace assignments

fix: set _current_streaming_track_id eagerly to prevent duplicate detection

Track change was detected twice because _current_streaming_track_id
was only set inside get_audio_stream loop (async, delayed). The echo
from update_playing_status arrived before the stream loop picked up
the track, causing duplicate prebuffer start and wasted API call.

Fix: set _current_streaming_track_id immediately in _activate_playback
when track change is detected. The stream loop re-confirmation at
line 227 is kept as a safety guard.

commit.message:
feat: add switch/case/default statement (#41)

* feat: add switch/case/default statement

- Add COLON, CASE, DEFAULT, SWITCH token types
- Scan ':' as COLON; extend keyword trie for case/default/switch
- Store switch subject as hidden local '(switch)' to avoid DUP opcode
- Each case arm: GET_LOCAL + EQUAL + JUMP_IF_FALSE chain, no fall-through
- Multi-value arms (case 1, 2:) compile to an equality-chain
- Reuse LoopContext with start=-1 as switch sentinel for break/continue
- continue skips switch contexts to find nearest enclosing real loop
- emitLoopCleanup(int targetLocalCount) parameterized for correct cleanup
